elements.name: Description
texts: Knox describes the scattered bloodied rags showing traces of the bodily rigours of the pilgrimage

elements.name: Source
texts: Pastoral Annals. By an Irish Clergyman [i.e. James S. Knox], p. 384

elements.name: Publisher
texts: R. B. Seeley and W. Burnside, London

elements.name: Contributor
texts: Digitised by Google, sponsored by Princeton Library

elements.name: Text
texts: "The chapel was deserted, and the altar removed; nothing remained to commemorate the recent presence of a multitude, but an incredible quantity of rags, scattered here and there, in indescribable profusion, over the entire surface of the island; they seemed to have been town from the dress of the crowd, and testified with sanguinary fidelity what fearful lacerations the skins of the wearers must have undergone. The boatmen had withdrawn to gather the rags - bead-stringing being their episode, rag-gathering their vocation."
